meta.title: Dwarf Fortress guide and review corpus for web-remake AI coding
meta.purpose: Collect, organize, and abstract from-zero guides and player-review observations into actionable design/coding knowledge.
meta.copyright_practice: This corpus stores paraphrased summaries, source cards, URLs, and design abstractions rather than reproducing full guide text.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Quickstart Guide
source_cards.freshness: current v53.15 page observed in 2026 search results
source_cards.trust_level: high for mechanics, medium for exact UI details because wiki pages can lag versions
source_cards.notes: This should be the backbone for a guided tutorial questline, but not the only source.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Tutorials
source_cards.freshness: living index; search result observed update in 2025
source_cards.trust_level: high as an index, source freshness varies by linked tutorial
source_cards.notes: Use as a directory; each linked article still needs version checks.

source_cards.title: PeridexisErrant's DF Walkthrough
source_cards.freshness: classic-era orientation, still widely recommended for fundamentals
source_cards.trust_level: high for conceptual flow, medium-low for exact Steam UI details
source_cards.notes: Very useful for AI because it gives a narrative order rather than isolated encyclopedia pages.

source_cards.title: PeridexisErrant 矮人要塞攻略（中文翻译）
source_cards.freshness: translation of classic guide; exact UI may not match current Steam edition
source_cards.trust_level: high for Chinese terminology mapping, medium for current mechanics
source_cards.notes: For a Chinese-speaking AI coding team, this is the most useful human-readable introductory corpus.

source_cards.title: Steam Community — Simple Dwarf Fortress Guide by Polarbark
source_cards.freshness: posted 2022; observed guide metadata updated 2025, but Steam marks it incompatible/needs caution
source_cards.notes: Useful to understand how non-expert players explain the game, but check against wiki before encoding rules.

source_cards.title: Steam Community — Dwarf Fortress Intermediate Guide: Strike The Earth Like a Master
source_cards.trust_level: medium-high for Steam-era onboarding and midgame organization
best_for: sequenced setup for a new fortress
source_cards.notes: A valuable bridge between minimal survival and a full fortress.

source_cards.title: PC Gamer — Dwarf Fortress beginner guide
source_cards.notes: Good for onboarding copy and simplified mental models.

source_cards.title: Explorminate — Dwarf Fortress Beginner's Guide: Learn to Strike the Earth Better
source_cards.freshness: Steam release era; still useful for worldgen/embark heuristics
source_cards.notes: Useful for designing a beginner-friendly embark recommendation system.

source_cards.title: Green Man Gaming — Dwarf Fortress Beginners' Guide, Tips for Getting Started
source_cards.notes: Not a rule reference; useful for tutorial tone.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Adventure mode quick start
source_cards.freshness: observed as a recent v53-era Adventure Mode beginner guide
source_cards.trust_level: high for current Adventure Mode orientation
source_cards.notes: Use when/if the web remake includes an Adventure-like mode or a world exploration debug mode.

source_cards.title: Kitfox / BlindiRL YouTube beginner tutorials
source_cards.freshness: includes Steam-era official tutorial videos; Adventure Mode guide observed around 2025 launch coverage
source_cards.notes: Best used by humans to inspect UI flow; not ideal for direct automated text extraction.

source_cards.title: Fangamer — Official Dwarf Fortress Guidebook
source_cards.freshness: official guidebook listing; page observed in 2026 search
source_cards.trust_level: high for scope/topics, but actual book content must be obtained separately
best_for: what an official beginner-to-intermediate curriculum includes
source_cards.notes: Product page only describes the book; do not infer detailed rules from it.

source_cards.title: 灰机 Wiki — 快速入门指南
source_cards.freshness: Chinese community reference; verify current-version coverage
source_cards.trust_level: medium-high for Chinese terminology and introduction
source_cards.notes: Good companion to English wiki; exact version status should be checked per page.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress 教学教程／参考资料收集中文翻译
source_cards.notes: Use as a Chinese reference catalog, not as current UI truth.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Work orders
source_cards.freshness: living v53-era wiki page, may need specific version checks
source_cards.notes: One of the most important references for an AI coding tool implementing the economy loop.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Stockpile design
source_cards.notes: Translate into heuristics: shorter hauling paths, input/output proximity, congestion reduction.

source_cards.title: DFHack Docs — Quickfort user guide
source_cards.freshness: DFHack 53.04-r1 versioned docs observed in 2026 search results
source_cards.trust_level: high for blueprint/tool concepts, not base-game mechanics
source_cards.notes: This is directly useful for a web remake because web UIs can natively support blueprint import/export.

source_cards.title: DFHack Docs — Quickfort blueprint library / Dreamfort
source_cards.trust_level: high for blueprint examples and automation patterns
source_cards.notes: Great for defining an internal test map / regression scenario.

source_cards.title: Steam Community Guides index for Dwarf Fortress
source_cards.trust_level: medium as index, individual guides vary
best_for: what players repeatedly need help with
source_cards.notes: Use for discovery; do not encode rules without cross-checking.

source_cards.title: Gcores — 《矮人要塞》新手指南：如何上手这款硬核到极致的游戏
source_cards.trust_level: medium for vibe, lower for current UI
source_cards.notes: Very useful for explaining why the game feels special.

source_cards.title: Pixelated Playgrounds — A Review of Dwarf Fortress Reviews
source_cards.source_type: critical essay analyzing Steam review stories
source_cards.notes: Excellent for converting community anecdotes into UI/story-system requirements.

source_cards.title: Third Coast Review — Dwarf Fortress Is Breathtakingly Complex
source_cards.notes: Use as perception data rather than rule source.

source_cards.title: Washington Post — Dwarf Fortress review
source_cards.notes: Useful for simple high-level descriptions.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Bloodline: Boatmurdered
source_cards.trust_level: medium for cultural impact, not current mechanics
source_cards.notes: Do not treat as a current mechanics spec; treat as a cultural design target.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Stupid dwarf trick
source_cards.freshness: living wiki, project examples from many versions
source_cards.notes: Each trick depends on systems the remake may not initially implement.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Megaproject
source_cards.notes: Use to design achievements/blueprint examples, not early MVP.

source_cards.title: PC Gamer — How cats get drunk in Dwarf Fortress
source_cards.notes: Excellent example of how low-level simulation can create memorable stories.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Bug Tracker — Cats dying for no reason / alcohol poisoning?
source_cards.notes: Convert into requirement: story events need traceability from rule interaction to visible consequence.

source_cards.title: Steam discussion — Are all crazy stories genuine?
source_cards.notes: Use only as qualitative evidence; forum pages are noisy and mutable.

source_cards.title: Steam discussion — How do you get these cool stories?
source_cards.notes: Key lesson: the game does not always narrate automatically; players actively assemble stories.

source_cards.title: Dwarf Fortress Wiki — Stories
source_cards.freshness: living v53-era wiki page, observed in 2026 search results
source_cards.trust_level: medium-high as a story index, individual linked stories vary
source_cards.notes: Use as a directory for human reading and inspiration; not a mechanics spec.

source_cards.title: Reddit — How are you guys seeing all the stories?
source_cards.notes: Useful because players explicitly explain how they inspect the game to find stories.

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 学习心态：把失败当成学习回路
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 接受没有标准胜利条件，第一座堡垒的目标只是建立一个可持续小社区。
ui_requirements: 展示可恢复的失败摘要
提示玩家每次失败会留下世界历史
common_pitfalls: 把首次堡垒当成必须完美通关
过早追求复杂机器/岩浆/军事

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 创建世界
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 选择世界大小、历史年数、文明/站点/野兽/矿物/自然野性等参数。
ui_requirements: 显示危险度/资源丰富度评分
common_pitfalls: 新手选过大世界导致生成/运行慢
忽略自然野性和邪恶生物群系

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 选择启程地点 Embark
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 找一个安全、资源足够、可持续的地点，而不是一开始就挑战地狱难度。
ui_requirements: Embark Advisor：标红重/多层含水层、邪恶/凶恶区域、敌对邻居
推荐河流/树林/土层/非邪恶/非凶恶/可贸易文明
common_pitfalls: 不知道含水层会阻碍挖掘
缺木头或缺土层导致早期农业/床/桶困难
与哥布林/塔太近

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 准备七名矮人和起始物资
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 带足镐、酒、食物、种子、斧/医疗/贸易关键物资，并设置基础技能。
ui_requirements: 显示物资用途和缺失警告
推荐至少两名矿工与足够工具
common_pitfalls: 忘记镐导致无法挖掘
忘记斧头导致无法砍树
过早带大量无用奢侈品

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 抵达后建立安全聚集点和初始 stockpile
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 让矮人和物资尽快从野外进入相对安全的位置。
ui_requirements: 一键创建临时室内 All stockpile 和 meeting area
common_pitfalls: 以为能直接控制矮人
物资留在地表被动物/敌人/天气影响
把 refuse/corpses 放进主仓导致腐烂/瘴气

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 挖入口、楼梯、基础大厅
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 用 designation 间接下达挖掘命令，建立可扩展的垂直要塞骨架。
ui_requirements: 明确区分挖掘/通道/楼梯
common_pitfalls: 把 channeling 当普通挖掘
挖穿水体或让水对角流入
大厅过宽导致早期工程量过大

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 建立食物与酒的最小闭环
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 让矮人持续有食物和酒，优先种植 plump helmets 并建 still 酿酒。
ui_requirements: 提示禁止烹饪酒和种子
提供重复酿酒/做饭订单
common_pitfalls: 只关注食物忘记酒
把种子或酒拿去烹饪
农田太大浪费劳动力

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 建立第一批工坊和家具链
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 用 carpenter/stoneworker/mechanic 等工坊生产床、门、桌椅、箱、机制零件。
ui_requirements: 工坊邻近输入/输出 stockpile 的建议
显示缺材料/缺工人/缺技能原因
common_pitfalls: 工坊离材料太远导致搬运时间占满
没有足够木头做床/桶
关键技工被搬运打断

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 把通用仓库拆成专业 stockpile
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 建立 food/drink/seeds/wood/stone/bar blocks/refuse/corpses 等专用仓库。
ui_requirements: 高亮会腐烂/发臭/阻塞的物品
common_pitfalls: refuse 进主仓
石头堆占满所有空间
木头/石头搬运过远拖慢全堡垒

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 建立卧室、餐厅、办公室
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 从临时宿舍过渡到卧室/餐厅，给 manager/bookkeeper/broker 等管理员办公空间。
ui_requirements: 房间价值/归属/家具缺口面板
common_pitfalls: 没有办公室导致管理功能不顺
长期没有餐厅/卧室造成坏想法
房间设计过大占用过多工作量

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 启用 Manager 与 Work Orders
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 从手动工坊排队过渡到条件化、周期性生产。
ui_requirements: 订单产生的子任务追踪
common_pitfalls: 订单数量过大导致占用全堡垒
没有资源条件导致无限生产
bookkeeper 精度不足影响库存判断

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 贸易与商队
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 建立 trade depot、搬货、估价、买关键缺口物资。
ui_requirements: 显示 depot 是否 wagon 可达
标记精灵禁忌木/骨/壳制品
common_pitfalls: 贸易站路太窄
用禁忌材料激怒精灵
不知道哪些资源该买：酒/食物/种子/木头/桶/布/皮/金属等

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 早期防御：门、桥、机关、陷阱、狗、burrow
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 不追求完美军队，先保证可以封门、撤离、拖延敌人。
ui_requirements: 商队通路与陷阱阻塞检查
common_pitfalls: 陷阱/建筑挡住商队车路径
拉杆没连上桥
打仗时平民继续乱跑

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 金属工业与基础军事
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 生产武器、护甲、工具；训练小队以应对袭击和地下威胁。
ui_requirements: 矿石→条→武器/护甲链路图
缺燃料/缺 flux/缺矿石提示
common_pitfalls: 不知道金属需要燃料
有矿石但没有可用冶炼链
小队没有装备/训练/兵营

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 医院、清洗、肥皂链
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 在中期准备 hospital 及 thread/cloth/splints/crutches/plaster/buckets/soap/water 等物资。
common_pitfalls: 没有医生/床/桌/牵引床/水桶
肥皂链太长没提前准备
伤员无人清洗/包扎

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: 社会空间：酒馆、神庙、图书馆、公会
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 满足社交、信仰、学习、表演、访客等中后期需求。
common_pitfalls: 酒馆带来快乐也带来斗殴
访客和居民权限不清
缺杯/乐器/书架等配套

canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.name: Legends/故事观察与事后复盘
canonical_zero_to_fortress_flow.player_goal: 把玩家的建筑、失败、事故、英雄事迹沉淀成可查询的世界历史。
ui_requirements: 按人物/地点/物品反查故事
common_pitfalls: 只记录日志但没有检索/归因
玩家看不到因果链，只觉得随机

design_insights.name: 攻略不是线性答案，而是把玩家带入自给自足循环
design_insights.observation: 最有价值的新手攻略都围绕“从车队到可持续堡垒”展开：地点、物资、挖掘、仓库、食物、酒、床、餐厅、工坊、贸易、防御。
design_insights.implementation: 把 tutorial 做成一条可跳过的任务链；每个任务同时解释玩家意图、系统条件和失败原因。

design_insights.name: 间接控制是核心手感
design_insights.observation: 玩家通常不直接操控单个矮人，而是画区域、指定任务、配置规则；乐趣来自系统自动执行和出错后的调查。
design_insights.implementation: Job 系统必须包含 job reservation、labor eligibility、pathfinding、resource availability、cancellation reason，并在 UI 里可追踪。

design_insights.name: 物流和距离是隐藏主玩法
design_insights.observation: 很多攻略反复强调工坊、仓库、输入输出距离、wheelbarrows、垂直布局；这说明“少走路”本身就是策略。
design_insights.implementation: 引擎中每个 job 都应有移动成本；UI 提供物流热力图和 stockpile-link 可视化。

design_insights.name: Work Orders 是从新手手动操作到堡垒自动化的分水岭
design_insights.observation: 手动工坊队列适合教学，条件化 work orders 适合稳定中期运营。
design_insights.implementation: 早期就实现一个简化版条件订单系统：目标物品、数量、周期、条件、失败原因、生成 job 列表。

design_insights.name: 玩家故事需要可读的因果链
design_insights.observation: 评论中的精彩故事往往来自系统间交互；玩家需要能从尸体、伤口、日志、思想、关系、地点历史中还原事件。
design_insights.implementation: 每个重要事件都写入 EventLog，并保留 cause/ref links：actor、target、item、location、material、previous_event。

design_insights.name: 失败不是惩罚，是内容生成器
design_insights.observation: 水淹、含水层、怪物、斗殴、贵族要求、医疗失败、敌袭、物流崩溃都可以变成战报。
design_insights.implementation: 实现 Reclaim/Retire/Legends-lite：堡垒失败后仍保留废墟、幸存者、神器、墓碑和事故时间线。

design_insights.name: 复杂系统只有在可检查时才有魅力
design_insights.observation: 游戏魅力来自“几乎每个 tile、物品、生物、关系、念头都能检查”，但新手也常被信息埋没。
design_insights.implementation: 实现分层 inspector：默认展示三行结论，展开后展示完整状态、历史、规则来源和可行动建议。

design_insights.name: “愚蠢的矮人戏法”和巨构是后期目标替代品
design_insights.observation: 由于没有硬性胜利，玩家会自创金字塔、水泵、岩浆装置、巨型宫殿、Moria、机器等目标。
design_insights.implementation: 不要只做生存；提供 blueprint、成就、材料统计、工程验证和性能安全阈值支持自定义目标。

emergent_examples.name: 醉猫 / 酒精清洁链
emergent_examples.summary: 猫踩过溢出的酒，清洁身体时摄入酒精；体型/剂量/综合征规则共同造成醉猫甚至死亡的经典故事。
emergent_examples.coding_takeaway: 涌现故事来自小规则组合；每一步都应可在 debug/story trace 中查看。

emergent_examples.name: Boatmurdered
emergent_examples.summary: 早期著名接力战报，包含大象、洪水、岩浆、商队事故、精神崩溃等多重失败链。
emergent_examples.coding_takeaway: 不要写固定剧情；要让失败链、环境危险和玩家工程事故能自然叠加。

emergent_examples.name: 金字塔、水泵与血水表演
emergent_examples.summary: 玩家用采矿、冶炼、建筑、泵、水/血流动和神殿设定创造高度个人化的工程艺术。
emergent_examples.coding_takeaway: 系统应允许玩家把实用机制拼成非实用表达；不要把建筑限制成固定房间模板。

emergent_examples.name: 医院与肥皂链
emergent_examples.summary: 中期医疗不是单一按钮，而是一组床、诊断、清洗、手术、缝合、包扎、接骨、牵引、肥皂、桶、水源、布料等资源链。
emergent_examples.coding_takeaway: 复杂系统应做成可分解 pipeline；UI 必须显示缺哪一步。

emergent_examples.name: Quickfort / Dreamfort
emergent_examples.summary: 社区通过蓝图工具把完整要塞设计、仓库、房间、工坊、work orders 等结构化复用。
emergent_examples.coding_takeaway: Web 版应把蓝图 DSL 做成一等公民：导入、预览、旋转、差异检查、撤销、版本化。

implementation_backlog.title: GuideCorpusSourceCards
implementation_backlog.description: 把本 JSON 的 source cards 放进项目知识库，供 coding agent 检索。
acceptance: docs warn old sources are inspirational only

implementation_backlog.title: FirstFortressTutorialQuestChain
implementation_backlog.description: 用 F00-F12 实现从世界创建到基础防御的引导任务链。
acceptance: each tutorial step has player goal

implementation_backlog.title: EmbarkAdvisor
implementation_backlog.description: 根据 biome/aquifer/trees/water/soil/minerals/neighbors 生成新手可读的地点建议。
acceptance: shows why a site is recommended

implementation_backlog.title: JobReservationAndCancellationReasons
implementation_backlog.description: 实现间接控制核心：designation 产生 jobs，jobs 预定物品/建筑/位置/劳动力，失败要可解释。
acceptance: UI lists why job is suspended/cancelled
pathfinding failure is distinct from material failure

implementation_backlog.title: StockpileRulesAndLogisticsHeatmap
implementation_backlog.description: 实现专业仓库、过滤器、输入输出距离和搬运可视化。

implementation_backlog.title: SimplifiedWorkOrders
implementation_backlog.description: 实现 manager-lite：周期性订单、库存条件、资源阈值、任务展开。

implementation_backlog.title: StoryTraceEventLog
implementation_backlog.description: 用事件溯源记录人物、物品、材料、位置、伤口、关系和历史原因。
acceptance: every major event has cause links
postmortem groups events into story beats

implementation_backlog.title: BlueprintDSL
implementation_backlog.description: 借鉴 Quickfort 思路，设计 Web 原生蓝图格式。

implementation_backlog.title: HospitalPipeline
implementation_backlog.description: 以中期指南为依据，实现简化医院资源链和治疗阶段。

implementation_backlog.title: EmergentStoryRegressionScenarios
implementation_backlog.description: 把醉猫、洪水、酒馆斗殴、商队路径堵塞、肥皂链缺口等做成测试场景。

coding_prompts.name: 实现新手引导链
coding_prompts.prompt: Read guide_source_cards G01/G03/G06 and flow steps F00-F12. Implement a tutorial quest chain where each step has goal, prerequisites, success detector, cancellation/failure hints, and optional skip. Do not hardcode Dwarf Fortress names beyond our own data model; use generic colony-sim wording where needed.

coding_prompts.name: 实现 Embark Advisor
coding_prompts.prompt: Using flow F01-F03, implement an EmbarkAdvisor that scores a candidate site by aquifer risk, trees, surface water, soil/clay/sand, flux/iron, biome danger, neighbors, and trade access. Return machine-readable warnings and human-readable explanations.

coding_prompts.name: 实现 Work Order 条件系统
coding_prompts.prompt: Using G15 and flow F10, implement a simplified WorkOrder model: target item, quantity, repeat cadence, AND conditions, resource-counting query, generated jobs, and failure reasons. The UI should explain why an order is inactive or spam-producing too many jobs.

coding_prompts.name: 实现事件溯源与故事面板
coding_prompts.prompt: Using design insights D05-D07 and examples E01-E02, implement EventLog entries with actor/target/location/item/material/cause_event references. Build an inspector that can answer: what happened here, why did this dwarf die, what caused this flood, and what is this item’s history?

coding_prompts.name: 实现 Quickfort-like 蓝图
coding_prompts.prompt: Using G17/G18 and backlog B08, design a JSON blueprint DSL that can describe multi-z-level dig/build/place/zone/stockpile/order commands with preview, validation, rotation, and undo.
